minor_participant_details.age_range_of_the_minors_participating_in: Participants will primarily be middle- and high-school students between 12–17 years old, with the majority clustered in the 13–16 range. A small number of particularly advanced 11-year-olds may attend specific morning sessions with parental consent, but we do not anticipate anyone younger than 11.
minor_participant_details.mitigation_measures_details_line_1: All program staff, including teaching assistants and volunteers, will complete a background check and mandatory training on working with minors, including boundaries, mandated reporting, and our code of conduct. At least two screened adults will be present with minors at all times; one-on-one unsupervised interactions between staff and youth are prohibited except in observable, interruptible spaces (e.g., glass-walled offices with doors open).
minor_participant_details.mitigation_measures_details_line_2: We will maintain a strict check-in/check-out protocol: minors must be signed in each morning and signed out at the end of the day by a pre-authorized adult listed on their registration form, or, for older teens with written parental permission, allowed to self-dismiss at a specified time. Attendance will be taken at the start of each session, after lunch, and before dismissal, and any discrepancies will trigger immediate follow-up calls to parents or guardians.
minor_participant_details.mitigation_measures_details_line_3: Physical safety measures include keeping all programming in a limited-access section of the building, using locked exterior doors with controlled entry, and clearly marked staff-only areas. We will maintain a minimum adult-to-student ratio of 1:8 in classrooms and 1:6 on any field trips, with head counts taken whenever the group moves between locations. First-aid kits and emergency contact binders will be present in every instructional space, and at least one staff member with current CPR/first-aid certification will be on site at all times.
minor_participant_details.mitigation_measures_details_line_4: In terms of health and COVID-related precautions, we will follow the current university and local public health guidelines in effect at the time of the program. Families will be asked to keep students home if they are experiencing fever, cough, or other signs of contagious illness. We will encourage good hand hygiene with scheduled hand-washing or sanitizing breaks (arrival, before snacks/meals, after restroom use, and after hands-on activities), provide hand sanitizer in all rooms, and increase ventilation by keeping windows open when feasible and using HEPA-filter air purifiers in enclosed classrooms. Masks will be optional but fully supported for any participant or staff member who chooses to wear one, and we will communicate all health expectations clearly to parents and guardians before the program begins.
